title: Cut out and Replay: A Simple yet Versatile Strategy for Multi-Label Online Continual Learning
key_challenges: Massive missing past and future labels in both data stream and memory buffer
Severe class imbalance that persists in the memory buffer
Pervasive missing labels: samples only annotated with labels from current task
Uncontrollable imbalanced classes following long-tailed distribution
Co-occurrence bias between head and tail classes
objective: Efficiently adapt the learning model to the current task while preventing catastrophic forgetting of previously learned tasks

previous_approaches.focus: Balancing head and tail classes in replay-based approaches
Reforms memory updates as a sample selection optimization problem
Generates predictions for previously seen classes while modeling dynamic label relationships
Combines dynamic pseudo-labeling for old classes with session-specific knowledge transfer

previous_approaches.method: Asymmetric Knowledge Distillation (AKD) and Online Relabeling (OR)
previous_approaches.focus: Tackles both missing labels and class imbalance simultaneously

limitations_of_previous_approaches: Feature learning mechanisms have inherent limitations with multi-label data
Conventional approach of extracting a single feature vector per example suffers from co-occurrence bias
Techniques like pseudo-labeling and resampling are particularly problematic with missing labels and class imbalance
